spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates coarse CPU-tier adaptation and now shows the server-side Client Hints negotiation used around that adaptation: Accept-CH, Critical-CH, Vary, Server-Timing, selected profile, and echoed Sec-CH-* request headers.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP loaded /v151/cpu-performance-api/server-adaptation-echo/?verify=after-critical-ch and clicked Run adaptive request. The live status changed to simulated / balanced / 3 received / 157ms, and the JSON output updated with response headers and request metadata.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: The Chrome 148 verification path used the simulated CPU-tier fallback because navigator.cpuPerformance is not present, while still surfacing received low-entropy Sec-CH headers and a null Sec-CH-Device-Memory value instead of hiding unsupported hints. The server work delay is now a real delayed response matching the Server-Timing value.

references.rationale: The concept page now embeds https://chromestatus.com/feature/5189864286978048 and the CPU Performance API draft in its references section.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

summary: Server-side adaptation now includes the Critical-CH/Accept-CH negotiation, echoes received Sec-CH-* request headers, shows a real delayed response with Server-Timing, and embeds the canonical ChromeStatus reference. Chrome DevTools MCP verification found no console errors; the new conformance route assertion passes, while the existing CPU API assertions fail only because the test browser is Chrome 148 and the feature targets v151.
